methods.description: Process for preparing shale, silty shale, laminated shale, and limestone samples for arcellinidan analysis.

steps.description: About 50 gm of each sample was thoroughly washed with distilled water, crushed into coarse sand size powder and treated with 5% hydrochloric acid (HCl) to remove carbonates.
steps.searchable_summary: Wash and crush samples, treat with HCl to remove carbonates

steps.description: Followed by a treatment with 5% hydrofluoric acid (HF) for 15 minutes to remove silica.
steps.searchable_summary: Treat samples with HF to remove silica

steps.description: Treat with 50% nitric acid and 5% potassium hydroxide (KOH) for oxidation and the removal of humic matter.
steps.searchable_summary: Oxidize and remove humic matter with nitric acid and KOH

steps.description: Residues were sieved through 15 µm sieves and divided into two parts, one for the preparation of optical slides and the other for scanning electron microscopy (SEM).
steps.searchable_summary: Sieve residues and divide for optical slides and SEM

methods.description: Preparation of optical slides for microscopic examination of arcellinidans.

steps.description: Residues were mixed with a polyvinyl alcohol solution and smeared over a coverslip for drying.
steps.searchable_summary: Mix residues with polyvinyl alcohol and smear on coverslip

steps.description: Coverslips were mounted on slides with Canada Balsam solution.
steps.searchable_summary: Mount coverslips on slides with Canada Balsam

steps.description: Slides were scanned under 200x magnification using an Olympus light microscope, model no. BX51 and photographs were taken at 400x or 1000x magnification.
steps.searchable_summary: Scan and photograph slides under microscope

methods.description: Preparation of samples for scanning electron microscopy to analyze arcellinidans.

steps.description: The processed sample was mixed with glycerin, spread over a glass slide and scanned under the Zeiss light microscope, model Axio Imager using 100x magnification.
steps.searchable_summary: Mix sample with glycerin and scan under microscope

steps.description: The desired grain was picked with the help of human hair attached to a dissecting needle.
steps.searchable_summary: Pick desired grain with dissecting needle

steps.description: The picked grain was mounted onto an SEM stub, and the grain was washed with alcohol to remove traces of glycerin.
steps.searchable_summary: Mount grain on SEM stub and wash with alcohol

steps.description: The prepared stubs were sputtered with gold-palladium and imaged with a JEOL JSM-IT300 Scanning Electron Microscope.
steps.searchable_summary: Sputter stubs with gold-palladium and image with SEM
